name: Metamorphosis Process
description: Metamorphosis Process in Amphibians/Insects. This process represents a higher-level biological mechanism distinct from molecular/biochemical processes covered in GLMP.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A5[Larval Stage] --> B5[Environmental Trigger]
    B5 --> C5[Hormonal Cascade]
    C5 --> D5[Cell Death Initiation]
    C5 --> E5[Cell Proliferation]
    D5 --> F5[Tissue Remodeling]
    E5 --> F5
    F5 --> G5[Organ Transformation]
    G5 --> H5[Limbs Development]
    H5 --> I5[Adult Features]
    I5 --> J5[Metamorphosis Complete]
    J5 --> K5[Adult Stage]
    
    style A5 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style B5 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style C5 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style D5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style E5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style F5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style G5 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style H5 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style I5 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style J5 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
    style K5 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Programming Framework: A Universal Process Visualization Methodology
sources.notes: Higher-level biological process visualization: Metamorphosis Process. Created using the Programming Framework methodology for organismal and ecological process analysis.

sources.title: Molecular mechanisms of amphibian metamorphosis: current understandings and future directions
sources.authors: Denver, R. J.; Shi, Y. B.

sources.title: The evolutionary and developmental origins of insect metamorphosis
sources.authors: Snyder, P. W.; Truman, J. W.

notes: Higher-level biological process. GLMP covers biochemical/molecular processes; this database covers organismal/ecological processes.
